plan_metadata.name: Feature Publish to Teams from Codelens

steps.description: Click on the "Teams Agents and Apps" option within the dropdown menu under the "New Project" dialog in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Other Teams Capability" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" option (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects) in the "Workspace Folder" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Use keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+` to toggle the terminal panel within the Visual Studio Code interface, focusing on executing tasks or viewing terminal output.
Use the keyboard shortcut `Ctrl+`` to toggle the integrated terminal in the Visual Studio Code interface, enabling or hiding the terminal panel located at the bottom of the screen.
Use keyboard shortcut "Ctrl+Shift+E" to focus on the Explorer panel in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension of Visual Studio Code.
Click on the file item "m365agents.yml" in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to select it.
Use the keyboard shortcut `ctrl+g` to open the "Go to Line" dialog in Visual Studio Code, allowing navigation to specific line numbers within the active file (`m365agents.yml`).
Type text: '92' into the column number input field in the Visual Studio Code interface to navigate to line 92 in the open file.
Click on line number "92" in the code editor within the VS Code interface, specifically targeting the "publish:" keyword in the "m365agents.yml" file.
@assertion notification "actions in publish stage executed successfully" exist
